text.title: E2E Testing Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules every Playwright/Cypress E2E suite MUST enforce. Hard rules — operator input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Follow the 70/20/10 pyramid: 70% unit, 20% integration, ≤10% E2E. Each E2E test MUST cover a complete user journey, not a single component.
rule.rationale: E2E tests are 10–100× more expensive to maintain than unit tests. Reserve them for journeys where unit + integration coverage cannot prove the contract.

rule.statement: Every page or major feature section MUST have a Page Object class. Page Objects expose actions and assertions through methods, NOT raw selectors.
rule.rationale: POM isolates selector churn from test logic. When the markup changes, one Page Object updates, not 40 test files.

rule.statement: Prefer role-based selectors (getByRole, getByLabel, getByPlaceholder). data-testid only when semantic selectors are not feasible. NEVER use CSS class selectors or XPath.
rule.rationale: Role selectors survive refactors and double as accessibility checks. CSS classes change with styling refactors and silently break the suite.

rule.statement: NEVER log in through the UI for every test. Run one global auth setup, save storageState to a file, and reuse it across all projects.
rule.rationale: UI login per test multiplies wall time by N and creates a shared rate-limit bottleneck on the auth endpoint. storageState makes auth a one-time cost.

rule.statement: Mock external APIs with page.route() to isolate E2E tests from third-party services. Mock ONLY external boundaries — your own API is exercised with real calls.
rule.rationale: External services produce nondeterministic failures (rate limits, outages, 502s). Mocking them at the network boundary keeps the suite green when only your code changed.

rule.statement: NEVER use page.waitForTimeout(N) / fixed sleep. Use expect().toBeVisible(), page.waitForURL(), or page.waitForResponse() instead.
rule.rationale: Fixed sleeps are flake amplifiers: too short on a slow CI runner → false fail; too long → wasted CI minutes. Auto-waiting locators are deterministic.

rule.statement: Use factory functions with faker to generate test data. NEVER hardcode user emails or IDs. Each test MUST set up its own state — no inter-test dependencies.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded data collides under parallel sharding. Shared state turns shard failures into mystery flakes that don't reproduce locally.

rule.statement: Shard suites larger than 50 tests across CI machines (--shard=N/total) and merge blob reports with `npx playwright merge-reports`. Keep total wall time ≤10 min.
rule.rationale: A 30-minute suite blocks PRs. 4-way sharding brings the same suite to 8 minutes for the cost of 4 CI minutes — strictly better.

rule.statement: For greenfield projects, the emitted config MUST select Playwright. Cypress remains supported only when migrating an existing Cypress suite.
rule.rationale: Playwright dominates new-project adoption: parallelism, network interception, multi-browser, and tracing are all first-class.

rule.statement: When `visual_regression_needed == true`, use toHaveScreenshot() on component-scoped locators only, with masked dynamic regions. NEVER full-page screenshots.
rule.rationale: Full-page snapshots break on every avatar/timestamp/ad. Component snapshots stay stable for months.

rule.statement: When `ci_machine_budget < 4`, the emitted workflow runs on a single runner with retries: 2 and trace: 'on-first-retry'.
rule.rationale: Sharding below 4 machines saves under a minute and adds blob-merge complexity. Single runner is the right default.
